item.title: Abe Foxman, former head of Anti-Defamation League, dies at 86
item.description: The ADL said in a statement that it "deeply mourns the loss of our longtime national director," without providing details about where and when Foxman died.

item.title: American on hantavirus ship tests "mildly" positive on flight home, HHS says
item.description: A Dutch-flagged cruise ship that was hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak reached Spain's Canary Islands early Sunday morning.

item.title: Crowds at Los Angeles Zoo go bananas for endangered species baby boom
item.description: The Los Angeles Zoo welcomed a baby gorilla, a baby orangutan and three baby chimpanzees in a span of less than five months. Joy Benedict reports on the baby boom of endangered species.

item.title: 5/10/2026: Prime Minister Netanyahu; Drawing the Lines; Gout Gout
item.description: First, Benjamin Netanyahu: The 60 Minutes Interview. Then, Gov. Landry on suspending Louisiana primaries. And, Gout Gout: The 60 Minutes Interview.

item.title: 5/10: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship headed back to the United States. Trump says Iran proposal "totally unacceptable."

item.title: Taiwan to be key issue at Trump's summit with China's President Xi
item.description: Taiwan, one of the world's biggest diplomatic flashpoints, will be top of mind for President Xi when he meets with President Trump.

item.title: Trump says Iran's response to peace proposal "totally unacceptable"
item.description: President Trump did not provide further details on what issues he had with the response or what would come next.

item.title: U.S.-Iran diplomacy stalls as fighting continues between Israel and Lebanon
item.description: President Trump on Sunday reacted to Iran's official response to the latest U.S. proposal to end the three-month old war. Meanwhile, fighting continues between Israel and Lebanon despite a ceasefire. Holly Williams reports from Tel Aviv.

item.title: What's at stake in Trump's trip to Japan, South Korea and China
item.description: President Trump will depart Washington on Monday for Asia. He'll make stops in Japan and South Korea before traveling to China to meet with President Xi Jingping for a two-day summit. Anna Coren is in Taipei with the details.

item.title: Body of soldier who went missing in Morocco recovered, U.S. Army says
item.description: The Moroccan military recovered the body of one of two U.S. soldiers who went missing on May 2, the U.S. Army said.

item.title: Americans on hantavirus cruise ship heading back home
item.description: Americans who were on a cruise ship hit by a deadly hantavirus outbreak are headed back to the United States. They were evacuated Sunday from the Canary Islands. Remy Inocencio is in Tenerife with the latest.

item.title: Search team finds body of missing U.S. soldier in Morocco
item.description: The U.S. Army announced on Sunday the remains of one of the two missing American soldiers in Morocco has been recovered.

item.title: Redistricting could prop up Republicans in the midterm elections
item.description: The battle over redistricting is swinging in favor of Republicans, giving them a built-in advantage to retain control of the House despite President Trump's record-low approval rating. Aaron Navarro is at the White House with more.

item.title: New video shows trespasser on Denver airport runway before deadly collision
item.description: A surveillance video shows the alleged trespasser on the runway at the Denver International Airport before a Frontier jet struck and killed the person.

item.title: Sen. Mark Kelly says Pentagon's $1.5 trillion budget request is "outrageous"
item.description: Democratic Sen. Mark Kelly of Arizona said the Trump administration's $1.5 trillion budget request for defense spending is "outrageous."

item.title: War with Iran is "not over," Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu says
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said the war with Iran is "not over" because there's still highly enriched uranium that needs to be removed from the country.

item.title: Netanyahu wants Israel "to draw down to zero the American financial support"
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu addresses the conflicts in Iran and Lebanon, the prospects for a peace deal, and the relationship between the U.S. and Israel.

item.title: Meet Gout Gout, the Australian sprinter drawing comparisons to Usain Bolt
item.description: Australian sprinter Gout Gout is breaking records, including one set by Usain Bolt. Track coach Di Sheppard first saw Gout run at age 12 and predicted: "I'm going to make that one a champion."

item.title: Louisiana's governor defends decision to suspend congressional primaries
item.description: After the Supreme Court ruled a Louisiana congressional map unconstitutional, Gov. Jeff Landry suspended primaries, the state is redrawing its map, and some Black voters fear losing their voice in D.C.

item.title: Full transcript of 60 Minutes' interview with Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu
item.description: Read the full transcript of Major Garrett's May 10, 2026 interview with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu here.

item.title: How the fastest teen ever in the 200m got his start on the track
item.description: After seeing Gout Gout run, Di Sheppard knew he was the real deal and wanted to coach him. In the years since, the teen has broken world records.

item.title: How Gout Gout stacks up against Usain Bolt in 200 meters
item.description: Correspondent Jon Wertheim breaks down the data behind 18-year-old Australian phenomenon Gout Gout's U20 world record 200-meter sprint, comparing it to Usain Bolt's standing 2009 world record, and what his coach said it will take to win Olympic gold.

item.title: Gout Gout: The 60 Minutes Interview
item.description: Australian sprinter Gout Gout is breaking records, including one set by Usain Bolt. Track coach Di Sheppard first saw Gout run at age 12 and predicted: "I'm going to make that one a champion."

item.title: Louisiana rushes to redraw congressional maps after Gov. Landry suspends primaries
item.description: After the Supreme Court ruled a Louisiana congressional map unconstitutional, Gov. Jeff Landry suspended primaries, the state is redrawing its map, and some Black voters fear losing their voice in D.C.

item.title: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu: The 2026 60 Minutes Interview
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu addresses the conflicts in Iran and Lebanon, the prospects for a peace deal, and the relationship between the U.S. and Israel.

item.title: How Gout Gout stacks up against Usain Bolt in 200 meters
item.description: Correspondent Jon Wertheim breaks down the data behind 18-year-old Australian phenomenon Gout Gout's U20 world record 200-meter sprint, comparing it to Usain Bolt's standing 2009 world record, and what his coach said it will take to win Olympic gold.

item.title: Coast Guard seizes sailboat in Lynette Hooker's disappearance probe, sources say
item.description: The sailboat used by Brian and Lynette Hooker​ in their travels around the Bahamas — named "Soulmate" — has been seized by U.S. Coast Guard investigators.

item.title: International operation disrupts "cocaine highway" across the Atlantic
item.description: Europol said an international operation successfully disrupted a major drug trafficking route known as the "cocaine highway."

item.title: Motherless daughters: Coming together
item.description: Twenty years ago, Hope Edelman, author of the bestseller "Motherless Daughters," founded a global support network for women who, like her, were young when their mothers died – to share tears, sisterhood and affirmation.

item.title: Supreme Court ruling ushers in a new era of gerrymandering
item.description: The Supreme Court has ruled that, under the Voting Rights Act, Congressional districts can no longer be drawn along racial lines, but can be shaped by partisan aims. The result: A dash to re-draw voting districts in several states. What does this portend for democracy?
